explanation: Verb stem + かけ marks an action that was started but left unfinished: 食べかけのパン is bread someone began eating and abandoned. As a verb, 〜かける means to begin doing something (and stop midway) or to be on the verge of doing it (言いかける = start to say). It is the mirror image of 〜きる, which stresses carrying an action through to the very end.
formation: V-ます stem + かけ（の N）／かける

examples.jp: テーブルに<ruby>食<rt>た</rt></ruby>べかけのパンがある。
examples.en: There's a half-eaten piece of bread on the table.

examples.jp: <ruby>読<rt>よ</rt></ruby>みかけの<ruby>本<rt>ほん</rt></ruby>が<ruby>机<rt>つくえ</rt></ruby>の<ruby>上<rt>うえ</rt></ruby>に<ruby>置<rt>お</rt></ruby>いてある。
examples.en: A book I'm partway through is sitting on the desk.

examples.jp: <ruby>手紙<rt>てがみ</rt></ruby>を<ruby>書<rt>か</rt></ruby>きかけて、<ruby>電話<rt>でんわ</rt></ruby>に<ruby>出<rt>で</rt></ruby>た。
examples.en: I started writing a letter, then answered the phone.

examples.jp: <ruby>彼<rt>かれ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>か<ruby>言<rt>い</rt></ruby>いかけて、<ruby>急<rt>きゅう</rt></ruby>に<ruby>黙<rt>だま</rt></ruby>ってしまった。
examples.en: He started to say something, then suddenly went quiet.
